region_of_origin: Africa / Asia (widely cultivated throughout Southeast Asia; staple in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Fresh leguminous vegetable — thin, dark green pods (Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis), ends trimmed, cut into pieces; raw preparation

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has limited direct coverage of yard-long beans as a distinct entry. FOODON_03301710 (yard-long bean) is used as the closest defensible parent under legume pod vegetables. Raw preparation state is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Yard-long beans (Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis) — raw, fresh pod vegetable
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers legume vegetables including yard-long beans as a pod vegetable. Used as closest defensible parent; exact subspecies code not separately enumerated in FoodEx2 v2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No confirmed SNOMED CT code for yard-long bean / Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis as a food substance. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 1 cup of cut long beans (roughly 100g), as commonly portioned for stir-fries or side dishes at Singapore hawker stalls and home cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Long Beans — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database + FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and FAO/INFOODS SEA
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database for long beans (raw) cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition table. Both are approved primary sources for Singapore and Southeast Asian foods. Values are consistent across both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Legume Vegetables — FAO/INFOODS SEA Reference Values and University of Sydney GI Database analogous legume pod entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / FAO/INFOODS SEA
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of approximately 30 (low) is consistent with published values for legume pod vegetables in the University of Sydney GI Database and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference tables. Yard-long beans are a leguminous pod vegetable with high fibre and moderate protein, supporting a low GI classification. GL per 100g serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (30 × 8.4) / 100 ≈ 2.5. Confidence 0.9 — no exact direct GI measurement for Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis found in the University of Sydney database; value is a well-supported analogous estimate from legume pod vegetable category.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (4mg/100g); high potassium (240mg/100g) and magnesium (44mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Long beans are an excellent choice for hypertension patients — extremely low sodium at 4mg/100g combined with 240mg potassium and 44mg magnesium per 100g actively supports blood pressure regulation through the DASH dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~30), low GL per serving (~2.5), high dietary fibre (2.9g/100g), plant protein (3.4g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Long beans are highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management — the low glycemic index (~30) and low glycemic load (~2.5 per 100g serving) minimise postprandial glucose excursions, while dietary fibre (2.9g) slows carbohydrate absorption and plant protein (3.4g) contributes to satiety without glycemic penalty.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, high potassium (240mg/100g), dietary fibre (2.9g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Long beans are cardioprotective — negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, and good dietary fibre (2.9g/100g) support a heart-healthy diet, while potassium (240mg/100g) and magnesium (44mg/100g) contribute to vascular health and normal cardiac function.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High folate (62mcg/100g), magnesium (44mg/100g), vitamin A (65mcg RAE/100g), iron (0.5mg/100g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Long beans are beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis patients — folate (62mcg/100g) is clinically important for patients on methotrexate therapy, magnesium supports anti-inflammatory enzyme pathways, and beta-carotene (vitamin A 65mcg RAE) provides antioxidant protection relevant to RA-associated oxidative stress.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Long Beans, also known as Yard-Long Beans (豇豆 / kacang panjang), are one of Singapore's most versatile and affordable wet market vegetables. They are the fresh, raw pods of Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis — thin, dark green leguminous pods that grow up to 60–90cm in length, sold in bundles at virtually every Singapore wet market year-round. The pods have a crisp, crunchy texture when raw and a mild, slightly sweet flavour. They are typically trimmed at both ends and cut into 5cm pieces before cooking. Long beans are a staple of Singapore Chinese stir-fries (commonly with garlic and dried shrimp or with sambal belachan in Malay cooking), and appear in Indonesian gado-gado and throughout Southeast Asian cuisines. Nutritionally, long beans stand out among vegetables for their relatively high protein content (3.4g/100g — attributable to their legume classification), good dietary fibre (2.9g/100g), and notable folate (62mcg/100g). Energy is low at 47 kcal/100g. For Type 2 Diabetes patients, long beans are excellent: low GI (~30), low GL per serving (~2.5), good fibre slowing carbohydrate absorption, and plant protein for satiety. For hypertension, the very low sodium (4mg/100g) combined with high potassium (240mg/100g) and magnesium (44mg/100g) makes them ideal. For rheumatoid arthritis patients on methotrexate, the folate content (62mcg/100g) is a direct clinical benefit. Widely available at Singapore wet markets at very low cost, long beans are an important affordable nutrition recommendation for patients across all income groups.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary approved source for Singapore foods) cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition table. Both are approved primary sources. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and consistent. GI is a well-supported analogous estimate from legume pod vegetable category with confidence 0.9. Multilingual coverage complete for Singaporean cuisine category.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for raw long beans not separately reported in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary sources consulted (HPB Foodo and FAO/INFOODS SEA). trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source for this raw vegetable. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived components. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant food; vitamin B12 is not synthesised by plants. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Foodo for raw long beans. GI value of ~30 is an analogous estimate based on legume pod vegetable category values from the University of Sydney GI Database and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference tables; no direct GI measurement for Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis was found in the University of Sydney database as of April 2026. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 used as closest parent (yard-long bean); exact raw preparation state not separately coded. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible parent for legume pod vegetables. SNOMED CT code left null — no confirmed code identified for this food substance.
